prompt.content:
Our client, HappyHospital, has two empanelled EHR/IT providers, and all IT & EHR expense is the final billed amount for maintaining the EHR system.
Q1. In 2025, these providers gave a combined $5,000,000 discount. Calculate the client’s total 2025 discount percentage and report the names of both providers, assuming each one’s discount rate equals this overall percentage.
A market analysis file lists facilities, providers, discounts, and an “Exclusivity Flag.” Use the updated mapping to correct this flag. If a facility is missing from the mapping, set Exclusivity = 1 when only one provider is empanelled (exclusive) and 0 when multiple providers are empanelled (non‑exclusive).
Q2. After updating exclusivity, compute the arithmetic‑mean discount percentage for each provider using only facilities with Exclusivity = 0.
Q3. Compare the 2025 discount percentage from Q1 with each empanelled provider’s average non‑exclusive discount from Q2 and identify which empanelled provider(s) give higher discounts to non‑exclusive facilities than to HappyHospitals.

Assumptions:
Round all final numbers to two decimal places.
Provide all results as console messages.​

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
1. Discount offered by the two empanelled providers in FY25 was 5.88%. These providers are Medisoft and Caresphere.

2. Average discount percentages offered by various providers (Non-Exclusive partnerships)

AstraCore Software	| 5.83%
BlueQuark Technologies	| 5.96%
CareSphere	| 6.07%
CrestEdge Solutions	| 6.01%
Medisoft	| 6.22%
NimbusLogic Systems	| 6.04%
SynapseWave Labs	| 5.85%

3. Discount offered to market for non exclusive partnerships, by CareSphere (6.07%) and Medisoft (6.22%) is higher than what our client got by the same empanelled providers in FY25 (5.88%)
